description: Kudurru boundary-stone scope must stay anchored to inscribed land-grant monuments, not generic survey stones or formal scribal institutions.

checks.reason: Seattle Art Museum dates a kudurru boundary stone to ca. 1300 BCE.
Kudurru commemorate land grants inside a broader land-tenure and boundary-marker practice.
The scoped monuments are inscribed kudurru records and charters.
The node is not scoped to general field-survey measurement tools.
Formal scribe schools are broader social infrastructure, not the direct prerequisite for the monument.
